Title: The occurrence of potato common scab correlates with the community composition and function of the geocaulosphere soil microbiome

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phytotoxin causing potato common scab symptoms
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Potato tuber lesions / geocaulosphere infection site
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Bacterial abundance highest in rhizosphere, but only geocaulosphere showed significant difference between high and low scab groups
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR copy number measurement across soil compartments
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S amplicon sequencing and qPCR, SRA BioProject PRJNA477767
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field comparison of high- versus low-scab severity plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bacterial 16S copy number was used to estimate bacterial abundance. 2. Only geocaulosphere soil showed a significant difference between high- and low-scab groups. 3. Higher bacterial abundance in geocaulosphere soil positively correlated with scab severity.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher bacterial abundance in geocaulosphere soil is associated with higher common scab severity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for microbiome load estimation in disease monitoring, but not a direct engineering target
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a community abundance marker, not a functional disease gene.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phytotoxin causing potato common scab symptoms
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Potato tuber lesions / geocaulosphere infection site
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Relative abundance increased in high-scab geocaulosphere microbiome
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field comparison of high- versus low-scab severity plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nrtABC was among nitrogen metabolism genes enriched in high-scab geocaulosphere samples. 2. The authors infer involvement in nitrate/nitrite transport. 3. Enrichment may contribute to conditions favoring thaxtomin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially associated with disease-conducive nitrogen metabolism in high-scab soils
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential soil functional biomarker for disease risk assessment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Community-level functional signal rather than a single validated organismal gene.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phytotoxin causing potato common scab symptoms
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Potato tuber lesions / geocaulosphere infection site
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Relative abundance increased in high-scab geocaulosphere microbiome
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field comparison of high- versus low-scab severity plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nasDEF was enriched with the nitrogen metabolism pathway in high-scab geocaulosphere samples. 2. Authors infer nitrate/nitrite reduction-related activity. 3. This enrichment may support thaxtomin-related pathogenic metabolism.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with high-scab microbiome nitrogen metabolism

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phytotoxin causing potato common scab symptoms
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Potato tuber lesions / geocaulosphere infection site
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Relative abundance increased in high-scab geocaulosphere microbiome
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field comparison of high- versus low-scab severity plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cynAB was listed among nitrogen metabolism functions enriched in high-scab samples. 2. It indicates altered nitrogen-associated transport/metabolism in the disease-conducive microbiome. 3. Authors relate this pathway enrichment to thaxtomin biosynthesis support.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with high-scab microbiome nitrogen metabolism

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phytotoxin causing potato common scab symptoms
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Potato tuber lesions / geocaulosphere infection site
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Relative abundance increased in high-scab geocaulosphere microbiome
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field comparison of high- versus low-scab severity plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. napB was enriched in the nitrogen metabolism pathway in high-scab geocaulosphere microbiomes. 2. The authors infer participation in nitrate/nitrite reduction. 3. Such functions may contribute to conditions favoring thaxtomin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with high common scab severity through enriched nitrogen metabolism

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phytotoxin causing potato common scab symptoms
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Potato tuber lesions / geocaulosphere infection site
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Relative abundance increased in high-scab geocaulosphere microbiome
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field comparison of high- versus low-scab severity plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Formamidase was among nitrogen metabolism functions enriched in the high-scab group. 2. The authors infer hydrolysis of formamide-related substrates. 3. Enrichment supports a disease-associated nitrogen metabolic profile potentially linked to thaxtomin biosynthesis.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phytotoxin causing potato common scab symptoms
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Potato tuber lesions / geocaulosphere infection site
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Relative abundance increased in high-scab geocaulosphere microbiome
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic KEGG functional enrichment of bacterial secretion system genes
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field comparison of high- versus low-scab severity plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple type III secretion-related genes were enriched in high-scab geocaulosphere microbiomes. 2. These genes were interpreted as pathogenicity-related functions. 3. Their enrichment suggests increased microbial capacity to interact with hosts or other microbes in disease-conducive soil.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with higher common scab severity via enriched pathogenicity-related secretion functions
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No single species assignment or direct validation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Community-level functional enrichment, not a single focal gene in one organism.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phytotoxin causing potato common scab symptoms
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Potato tuber lesions / geocaulosphere infection site
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field comparison of high- versus low-scab severity plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. K00799/gst was the second most abundant differential KO enriched in high-scab samples. 2. It mapped to glutathione metabolism and xenobiotics biodegradation/metabolism. 3. This suggests enhanced detoxification/xenobiotic-related functions in the disease-conducive microbiome.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with high-scab microbiome functional state
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential functional biomarker for microbiome state
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly tied to thaxtomin synthesis experimentally.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phytotoxin causing potato common scab symptoms
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Potato tuber lesions / geocaulosphere infection site
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Most abundant differential KO; enriched in high-scab geocaulosphere microbiome
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic KO abundance comparison and KEGG pathway assignment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field comparison of high- versus low-scab severity plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. K02035 was the most abundant differential KO category and was enriched in high-scab samples. 2. It was assigned as a member of the quorum sensing pathway. 3. The authors infer that quorum sensing-related functions may help coordinate disease-promoting microbial interactions.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with high-scab microbiome and putative disease-promoting quorum sensing
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential microbiome diagnostic marker for disease-conducive quorum-sensing activity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: KO-level function rather than a validated organism-specific gene.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Thaxtomin biosynthetic gene pair; nitration-related phytotoxin biosynthesis proteins
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Key pathway gene / committed phytotoxin biosynthesis step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Encodes core biosynthetic functions required for thaxtomin A phytotoxin production in pathogenic Streptomyces
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phytotoxin causing potato common scab symptoms
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Potato tuber lesions / geocaulosphere infection site
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in geocaulosphere soil associated with diseased tubers; much higher in high-scab samples
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR quantification of txtAB copy number; correlation with scab severity; metagenomic association with pathogenic Streptomyces abundance
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S amplicon sequencing and shotgun metagenomics, SRA BioProject PRJNA477767
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field conditions comparing high- versus low-common-scab potato plants
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key gene for thaxtomin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. txtAB was the only focal disease-metabolism gene directly quantified across compartments. 2. txtAB copies were detected clearly only in geocaulosphere soil and were significantly higher in the high-scab group. 3. txtAB copy number was strongly positively correlated with scab severity and pathogenic Streptomyces abundance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher txtAB abundance is associated with increased thaxtomin-related pathogenicity and more severe potato common scab
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful molecular marker for monitoring scab-pathogenic Streptomyces and disease risk in potato production soils
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper treats txtAB primarily as a biomarker of pathogenic Streptomyces and thaxtomin biosynthetic capacity rather than performing direct genetic manipulation.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: GS microbiome bacteria, potentially including cellulose-hydrolyzing taxa such as Variovorax and Stenotrophomonas
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cellulose degradation linked indirectly to thaxtomin A induction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes cellulose to release cellobiose-type products that may stimulate thaxtomin production by pathogenic Streptomyces
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phytotoxin causing potato common scab symptoms
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Potato tuber lesions / geocaulosphere infection site
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic KO detection plus literature-based inference discussed in paper
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field conditions comparing high- versus low-common-scab potato plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cellulose 1,4-beta-cellobiosidase K19668 was detected in the GS metagenome. 2. The authors used its presence to support the hypothesis that cellulose-hydrolyzing microbes may generate cellobiose/cellotriose. 3. These products could stimulate thaxtomin production and thereby promote common scab.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potential indirect promotion of thaxtomin production and common scab through cellulose breakdown products
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Inference only; no direct abundance comparison or gene manipulation shown
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential microbiome functional marker for disease-conducive soils, but requires validation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a hypothesized functional contributor rather than a directly validated core experimental gene.
